info.description: Failures on named entities, rare words, and terms absent from the training lexicon — manifesting as deletions, phonetic approximations, or garbled substitutions.
info.solution: Lexicon expansion with domain-specific wordlists; subword/BPE models (e.g. Whisper-style tokenization) to handle unseen surface forms; entity-biasing at decode time.

info.description: Substitutions driven by acoustic similarity: homophones, minimal phoneme pairs (e.g. b/p, d/t), vowel reduction errors, or phone insertions/deletions under noise.
info.solution: Acoustic model fine-tuning on minimal-pair augmented data; contrastive decoding with phoneme-level LM; noise-robust feature extraction (e.g. SpecAugment, multi-condition training).

info.description: Incorrect tokenization at word boundaries — merging adjacent words (e.g. 'in a' → 'inna') or splitting single words — caused by prosodic reduction or fast speech.
info.solution: Improved VAD and endpointing; CTC alignment tuning; training on spontaneous / fast-speech corpora to expose the model to boundary-blurred acoustics.

info.description: Deletion, insertion, or substitution of unstressed function words (determiners, prepositions, auxiliaries, pronouns) whose acoustic signal is weak or absent in connected speech.
info.solution: LM rescoring with stronger priors on grammatical function words; fine-tuning on read-aloud vs. conversational data to model reduction patterns.

info.description: Decoder LM overrides the acoustic signal in favour of high-frequency phrases or n-gram patterns — substituting a plausible word that sounds wrong acoustically.
info.solution: Reduce LM weight in the beam search; train or fine-tune a domain-specific LM; apply minimum Bayes risk (MBR) decoding to downweight over-confident hypotheses.

info.description: Errors normalizing spoken-form numbers, dates, units, acronyms, and alphanumeric tokens (e.g. 'twenty twenty-four' vs '2024'; 'NATO' vs 'nato').
info.solution: Post-processing inverse text normalization (ITN) rules or a learned ITN model; include formatted/spoken-form pairs in training data.

info.description: Systematic errors correlated with speaker identity — accent, dialect, pitch range, or speaking rate — producing consistent substitution patterns not explained by phoneme confusion alone.
info.solution: Speaker-adaptive fine-tuning (x-vector conditioning, LHUC); accent-diverse training data; test-time adaptation with a few utterances of the target speaker.

info.description: Spurious or missing tokens from hesitations, repetitions, self-corrections, false starts, or premature / delayed end-of-speech detection cutting off words.
info.solution: Separate disfluency detection layer post-ASR; endpointer tuning (patience window, silence threshold); train on spontaneous speech with disfluency annotations.
